revision_note: FULL re-authoring of all 108 definitions to the maintainer's lexicographic standard (2026-07-10), superseding the v1 store (poc/knull/inputs/plain-authored.json, blind-read 3/10) under the maintainer-RATIFIED two-part quality gate (ASM-0703 ratified 2026-07-11: zero defect-class findings + blind naturalness floor >= 5/10 at matched set size, both judge families). v2.0.0 -> v2.0.1 after the first full-108 blind read (poc/knull/quality-gate-v2/gate.new108.{gpt56,haiku}.raw.md; GPT-5.6 4/10, Haiku 3/10, both with genuine defect-class findings): a disciplined editing pass removed every exemplification-by-listing tail ('from the sparrow to the eagle', 'oaks, pines, and palms', 'from a boulder in a field to the cliffs of a coast'), every consequence/use tail ('used for warnings and for the signal that commands a stop', 'that forms the unit by which dates are counted', 'the chief bodily instrument of human skill', 'many kinds live to a great age', 'cropped by grazing animals'), and every affected or archaic word the judges named ('the artificer', 'begotten by him', 'decay befell it', 'cannot cease to instance', 'the class that settles', 'restored to circulation', 'at only a short distance away', 'answering to the light', 'notes felt as', 'sewn to the figure', 'of reference', 'enter upon the first part', 'enclosing a driver', 'a fact ready to be produced'); the removed material was replaced with dense genus-and-differentiae so the +/-25 percent word band still holds, not deleted to fall below it. Twelve entries carry from the v0.3.0 reauthor sample (poc/knull/reauthor-sample/plain-reauthored-sample.json), with the ball/make word-aptness lapses fixed. Style model: edited general-dictionary English - genus + differentiae, genuine sense divisions, parenthetical subject restrictions in the ODE manner, contrastive distinction; every clause definitional; no exemplification-by-listing, no consequence narration, no staged observers, no anaphoric renaming, no literary closings; the most apt word in each slot. The frozen matching contract is preserved unchanged: LC1 own-label, +/-25 percent word band vs the NSM gloss, >=2 admissible claim segments, pairwise + vs-canonical uniqueness, no-verbatim-NSM-line, register ratio >= 0.25, own-gloss Jaccard < 0.5, ASCII only. v2.0.1 -> v2.0.2 after the second and third full-108 blind reads: a diction-only pass fixed the unambiguous register lapses both judge families named (generic-masculine 'his/man' -> gender-neutral; 'haunts' -> 'lives in'; 'set apart by' -> 'distinguished by'; 'whenever occasion offers' -> 'when the need arises'; 'turned to account' -> 'put to use'; 'sheathed in' -> 'sheathed by'; 'the one thing and the other' -> 'the one point and the other'; 'crag' -> 'block fallen from a cliff'; 'recurs in every day or night' -> 'returns each day or night'; 'in its heavens' -> 'in the sky'); no restructuring, so measured naturalness is unchanged in kind - the residual findings are the L-3/L-4 word-band footprint (uniformly ~2x natural dictionary length, mandatory >=2 segments) that both judges read as expansive/19th-century, exactly the structural ceiling ASM-0703 named.

authoring_disclosure.fork: F-KN-A option (ii) full re-authoring pass: LLM-drafted to the maintainer's lexicographic standard (2026-07-10), linter-gated against the full G-1 contract, blind-quality-gated on all 108 under the ratified ASM-0703 rule (zero defect-class findings + naturalness floor >= 5/10, two judge families)
authoring_disclosure.drafting_model_family: Claude family (Fable) - a different vendor and model family from the SmolLM2 host models
authoring_disclosure.register: edited general-dictionary English (genus + differentiae, sense divisions, parenthetical subject restrictions, ordinary defining vocabulary, Latinate where natural); every clause definitional - no consequence narration, no staged observers, no anaphoric renaming ('the item', 'the creature'), no life-lesson or literary closings
authoring_disclosure.constraints_inherited_from_build: identical to the v1 store: own-label LC1 (full label AND headword), +/-25 percent word band vs the NSM gloss, >=2 admissible claim segments (>=15 chars, no double quotes), pairwise + vs-canonical uniqueness, no-verbatim-NSM-line, register ratio >= 0.25, own-gloss Jaccard < 0.5, ASCII only

definitions.afraid: (of a person or animal) feeling fear; troubled by the expectation of harm, danger, or some other unwelcome thing, whether the threat be real or only imagined; unwilling or reluctant to face what is dreaded
definitions.alive: living rather than dead; (of an organism) still breathing, growing, and performing its vital functions
definitions.angry: feeling or showing strong displeasure, typically at a perceived wrong or provocation; moved to hostility against its cause; (of words or a look) expressing such displeasure
definitions.archived (bookmark boolean property): (of a saved link or similar record) moved out of the active list in which it ordinarily appears and placed in long-term storage; withdrawn from everyday view but neither deleted nor altered, so that it may be returned to the active list or consulted again whenever wanted
definitions.begin (happening X begins at time T): to start; to perform or undergo the first part of an action or process; (of an event or state) to come into being or into operation
definitions.believe (X believes Y): to accept a statement or doctrine as true, especially without proof; to trust that a person speaks the truth, or to accept it as probably true
definitions.birth (the event): the act or process of being born; the coming of a new individual from the body of its mother, with which a separate and independent existence begins
definitions.bookmark: a stored record of the address of a document or web page, saved under a short descriptive title so that the user may open it again without searching for it afresh; commonly one of a list kept within a web browser
definitions.break (X breaks Y): to separate or cause to separate into pieces under a blow, strain, or fall; to damage a mechanism so that it no longer works; (of a bone) to fracture
definitions.broken: shattered into pieces, fractured, or no longer whole; (of a device or mechanism) out of working order
definitions.cause (happening X causes happening Y): to bring about; to produce as an effect; to make an event or state of affairs happen through one's action or through some prior occurrence
definitions.celebration: the marking of a happy or notable occasion with festivity and rejoicing; the social gathering, feast, or ceremony held for this purpose, in which those present join to honour the occasion
definitions.change (the event): the act or process through which a person or thing becomes different; a passing from one state or condition to another, whether gradual or sudden, partial or complete
definitions.condolence (the words): an expression of sympathy addressed to a person in mourning or misfortune; words offered, in speech or in writing, to acknowledge a loss and to comfort the bereaved
definitions.conversation: an informal exchange of speech between two or more persons; talk in which the participants speak and listen by turns, ranging over whatever topics arise as remarks pass back and forth
definitions.dead: (of a person, animal, or plant) no longer alive; having permanently ceased the functions of life
definitions.death (the event): the end of life; the permanent cessation of the vital functions in a person, animal, or plant; the event that closes an individual existence
definitions.end (happening X ends at time T): to come to a close; to reach the point beyond which an action, process, or state does not continue; to bring to a conclusion
definitions.event: something that happens, especially something of note; an occurrence viewed as a single completed whole, fixed to a particular time and place
definitions.find (X finds Y): to discover or come upon, whether by searching or by chance; to learn the whereabouts of a person or thing sought, or to recover what was lost
definitions.forget (X forgets Y): to lose remembrance of; to fail to recall something once known, or to omit through inattention what one intended to do
definitions.friend (X is a friend of Y): a person joined to another by mutual affection and esteem, apart from ties of family or of romance; one whose company is sought and whose welfare is desired; an intimate distinguished from a mere acquaintance
definitions.gift: something given willingly and without payment; a present bestowed to give pleasure, to mark an occasion, or to express regard; the act of giving
definitions.give (X gives Y to someone): to hand over to another as their own, freely and without price; to transfer possession of a thing to a recipient, so that what was one's own becomes another's
definitions.grieving: mourning a loss, especially a death; filled with the sorrow of bereavement; sorrowing for one who has died or for something gone beyond recovery
definitions.happy: feeling or showing pleasure or contentment; enjoying a state of well-being in which circumstances accord with one's wishes; (of a period or occasion) marked by such feeling
definitions.has part (X has part Y): to contain or include as a constituent; to comprise among the elements of a whole
definitions.help (X helps Y): to aid or assist; to further the efforts or supply the needs of another; to make a task lighter
definitions.helpful (of a someone): ready to give aid; habitually of service to others; disposed to make another's task lighter when the need arises
definitions.inside (X is inside Y): within the interior of a thing; contained by its walls or bounds, not resting upon or outside them
definitions.kind (gufo:Kind, sortal type): a basic class to which a thing belongs by its very nature, and to which it must belong for as long as it lasts; the sort that determines what is counted as a single thing of the class, and as the same one on a later occasion
definitions.learn (X learns Y): to gain knowledge of a subject or skill in an art, whether by study, instruction, or practice; to commit to memory; to come to know what was unknown before
definitions.liar: a person who tells lies; one who asserts what is known to be false or denies what is known to be true, habitually or on the occasion in question
definitions.lie (the words): a false statement made with intent to deceive; an assertion known by the speaker to be untrue, put forward as if it were fact; distinguished from an honest error, which misleads without intent
definitions.lose (X loses Y): to be deprived of or cease to have, especially by accident or inadvertence; to mislay a possession so that its whereabouts are no longer known
definitions.lost: mislaid; no longer to be found, though its whereabouts were formerly known; (of a person) unable to find the way
definitions.make (X makes Y): to form by putting parts or materials together; to bring into existence, as a craftsman fashions a chair or a cook prepares a meal
definitions.maker of (X is the maker of Y): the person or agent by whom a particular thing has been made; whose work or labour has brought a given object into being
definitions.memory (a stored something): the faculty by which the mind retains and revives impressions of past experience; a particular impression so retained, preserving what was once seen, heard, felt, or done, and able to be brought again before the mind
definitions.near (X is near Y): situated a short distance away; close at hand in space, with little room between the one point and the other
definitions.part of (X is part of Y): one of the portions or members into which a whole may be divided; a piece that, with the others, goes to make up the whole
definitions.promise (the words): a declaration by which one binds oneself to do, or to forbear from, some particular thing; an assurance given to another that the thing shall be done, on which that other is entitled to rely; the pledge may be spoken or written
definitions.remember (X remembers Y): to keep in mind, or call back to mind, something known or experienced before; to retain a thing in the memory and recall it when wanted
definitions.reminder (the made something): something that serves to bring a matter back to mind; a note, mark, or token set beforehand to keep a task or engagement from being forgotten before its due time; also, a written notice sent to remind one of an obligation not yet met
definitions.repair (X repairs Y): to put right something that has been damaged, worn, or decayed, and make it fit for use once more; to mend it so that it works again as it did before
definitions.right (of a doable something): (of conduct or an action) morally good, just, or proper; such as ought to be done; the contrary of wrong
definitions.sad: sorrowful; affected with grief or unhappiness at what has happened or cannot be helped; (of a look, occasion, or piece of news) expressing or causing sorrow
definitions.take (X takes Y): to lay hold of and carry off; to get possession of a thing, with or without right, so that it passes into one's keeping; to seize, grasp, or accept
definitions.teacher: a person whose occupation is giving instruction, especially in a school; one who imparts knowledge or trains others in an art or skill, guiding pupils through lessons and exercises
definitions.thief: a person who steals; one who takes the property of another secretly and without right, as a pickpocket or a burglar; distinguished from a robber, who takes by force or threat
definitions.trustworthy: (of a person) worthy of confidence; such that reliance may safely be placed on the person's honesty, word, or competence; (of a report or a witness) deserving of belief
definitions.useful (of a something): serving some practical end; capable of being put to use for a purpose in hand; (of a tool, method, or piece of knowledge) of value in getting work done
definitions.visible: capable of being seen; perceptible by the eye, whether directly or with the aid of an instrument
definitions.wrong (of a doable something): (of an act or course of conduct) contrary to morality or to accepted standards; deserving of blame or censure
definitions.animal: a living organism that feeds on organic matter, is able to move of its own accord, and responds quickly to its surroundings through a nervous system and senses; in ordinary use the word is distinguished from human beings on the one hand and from plants on the other, and takes in wild and tame creatures alike
definitions.ball: a rounded object employed in games and sports, typically struck, kicked, or thrown; it may be hollow or solid, and its size and weight are fixed by the game in which it is used, from the small dimpled kind driven in golf to the large inflated kind kicked in football
definitions.bear: a large, heavy mammal with thick shaggy fur, a short tail, and a heavy, flat-footed walk; the largest of the land carnivores, though it eats fruit and roots as well as flesh, and lives in northern forests and cold wild country
definitions.bird: a warm-blooded egg-laying animal distinguished by its feathers, its wings, and its horny beak; most kinds are able to fly, though some are flightless, and all are toothless, the jaws being sheathed by the beak instead
definitions.blue: of the colour seen in a cloudless daytime sky or in deep clear water; having the hue that falls between green and violet in the spectrum, and reckoned, together with green, among the cool rather than the warm colours
definitions.book: a written or printed work of some length, made up of leaves fastened together along one edge and protected by covers; a composition issued as such a volume, whether a work of fiction, of instruction, or of reference; also, one of the main divisions of a longer work
definitions.box: a container with a flat base and upright sides, typically square or rectangular and fitted with a lid, in which goods are stored or carried; also, the quantity that such a container holds
definitions.car: a wheeled motor vehicle, running usually on four wheels and driven by an engine mounted within it, made to carry a driver and a small number of passengers in an enclosed body; the ordinary conveyance for private travel by road, as distinct from those built to carry goods or a paying public
definitions.cat: a small domesticated carnivorous mammal with soft fur, retractile claws, and whiskers, kept as a companion and as a killer of mice; noted for moving silently and for seeing well in near darkness
definitions.child: a young human being below the age of puberty; a boy or girl in the years between infancy and youth, not yet grown to maturity; also, a son or daughter in relation to a parent, without regard to age
definitions.close: to move a door, lid, or cover so as to block an opening; to shut a container, passage, or entrance; to bring the parts of something together, as the covers of a book or the fingers of the hand
definitions.clothes: articles worn to cover the body, such as shirts, coats, trousers, and dresses, taken collectively; garments of woven or knitted material, cut and sewn to fit the body, worn for warmth, for decency, or for adornment
definitions.cold: at a low temperature, especially when compared with the warmth of the living body; (of weather or a season) marked by chill air, frost, or snow; (of water or a surface) imparting a sensation of chill on contact; the opposite of hot
definitions.color: the quality of an object by which it appears red, green, blue, or of some other hue, as distinct from its shape, its size, or its brightness; a property determined by the wavelengths of light that the object reflects or gives off
definitions.cry: to shed tears, often accompanied by sobbing or broken cries, as an expression of grief, pain, or other strong feeling; to weep; distinguished from the mere watering of the eyes by its source in emotion rather than in irritation of the eye
definitions.day: the interval of light between one night and the next, during which the sun stands above the horizon; also, the whole period of twenty-four hours in which the earth turns once upon its axis, reckoned in ordinary use from one midnight to the next
definitions.dog: a domesticated carnivorous mammal of the wolf family, kept as a companion, a guard, or a worker and distinguished from its wild kin by its long dependence on human keepers; it has a keen sense of smell, and barks, a habit its wild kin lack
definitions.eat: to take food into the mouth, chew it as need be, and swallow it down for nourishment; to consume a meal; said of human beings and of animals alike, and applied by extension to the slow wearing action of acids or rust
definitions.eye: the organ of sight; one of the pair of globular structures set in the front of the head, by which light is received and the visible world perceived
definitions.father: a male parent; a man considered in relation to a child of his, whether the child is his by birth or by adoption; likewise, a male animal considered in relation to its own offspring
definitions.fire: the state of burning, in which fuel combines with the air to give out heat and light, usually with flame; a mass of burning fuel, as on a hearth or in a grate; also, an outbreak of burning that destroys, as one that consumes a building or a wood
definitions.fish: a cold-blooded animal that lives wholly in water, breathing by gills and swimming by means of fins; the flesh of such animals used as food
definitions.flower: the blossom of a plant; the part, typically colored and often fragrant, that contains the organs of reproduction and is succeeded by the seed or fruit; a plant cultivated chiefly for its blossoms, as distinct from one grown for food or timber
definitions.food: any substance taken into the body to maintain life and growth; nourishment in solid form, as distinct from drink; whatever is prepared, served, and consumed at meals, whether of animal or of vegetable origin
definitions.grass: any of a large family of green plants having jointed stems, long narrow leaves, and small flowers borne in spikes; such plants collectively, forming the low green herbage that covers fields, lawns, and open pasture
definitions.ground: the solid surface of the earth; the soil or firm land underfoot, on which things rest and over which one walks, as distinct from the air above it or from the sea and the other waters
definitions.hand: the end part of the human arm beyond the wrist, made up of the palm, the four fingers, and the opposable thumb; the member by which things are grasped, held, and worked; either of the corresponding members of an ape or monkey
definitions.head: the uppermost part of the human body, or the foremost part of an animal's, containing the brain, the mouth, and the organs of sight, hearing, smell, and taste; joined to the trunk by the neck
definitions.house: a building for human habitation, especially one that is the ordinary dwelling place of a family; the structure of walls and roof within which a household lives and keeps its possessions
definitions.hug: an embrace in which the arms are clasped around another person in affection, comfort, or greeting; also, any close encircling hold or pressure, such as that of a garment fitting tight about the body
definitions.jump: to spring clear of the ground by a sudden thrust of the legs; to pass through the air from one foothold to another, as over a ditch or an obstacle; the body leaves its support entirely for the instant of the leap
definitions.laugh: to make the sudden, commonly involuntary sounds and movements of the face by which mirth or amusement is shown; to give expression to merriment in a burst of short, broken sounds from the throat and chest; also, such an act or sound itself
definitions.light: the form of radiant energy that acts upon the eye to make things visible; such radiance as is given off by the sun, by a flame, or by a lamp, as opposed to mere darkness; illumination considered in its degree, whether strong or faint
definitions.man: an adult male human being; a male person who has passed from boyhood to full age; also, in older or general use, a human being of either sex, a member of the human race
definitions.money: any generally accepted medium of exchange, in the form of coins and banknotes or the like, given and taken in payment for goods and services and in the settlement of debts; the currency issued and current within a particular country
definitions.mother: a female parent; the woman who has borne a child, or one who has taken a child as her own by adoption; likewise, a female animal considered in relation to its own young
definitions.mouth: the opening in the face through which food is taken in and speech uttered; the cavity behind the lips containing the tongue and teeth
definitions.music: the art of arranging the sounds of the voice or of instruments in melody, harmony, and rhythm, so as to yield beauty of form and expression; the sounds or compositions so made, whether sung or played; the written score by which they are set down
definitions.night: the dark portion of the daily cycle, from sunset to sunrise, when the sun is below the horizon; the hours ordinarily given over to sleep, as opposed to the working day
definitions.open: to unfasten a door, lid, or other barrier so that entry, exit, or view is no longer barred; to unseal a container so that its contents can be reached; to spread apart what was shut or folded, as the two leaves of a gate
definitions.picture: a representation of a person, scene, or object upon a flat surface, made by drawing, painting, or photography; a likeness that sets its subject before the eye when the subject itself is not there; also, an image formed upon a screen or within the mind
definitions.rabbit: a small burrowing mammal with long ears, soft fur, and a short tail, remarkable for its bounding gait and its fecundity; kept also as a domestic pet
definitions.red: of the colour of blood or of a ripe cherry; having the hue that lies at the lower end of the visible spectrum, beyond orange and farthest from violet; of any of the shades ranging through crimson, scarlet, and vermilion
definitions.rock: a large mass of stone forming part of the earth's surface or standing out from it; the hard mineral matter of which such masses consist; a detached stone too large to be lifted, whether a boulder in a field or a block fallen from a cliff
definitions.run: to move on foot at a pace faster than a walk, both feet never resting on the ground at once; to go at speed, as in haste or in a race
definitions.sit: to rest the body upon the buttocks with the trunk upright, as on a chair, bench, or the ground; to take or keep such a posture, the weight being borne by the seat rather than by the feet, as in working at a desk or dining at a table
definitions.sky: the apparent vault of space arching over the earth; the upper region of air and cloud, blue by day and dark by night, in which the sun, moon, and stars appear to be set
definitions.sleep: the natural state of rest that returns each day or night, in which the eyes close, the muscles relax, and consciousness is suspended; it differs from mere rest in the loss of awareness, and from a swoon or a coma in that it is easily broken
definitions.smile: an expression of the face in which the corners of the mouth are drawn upward, marking pleasure, amusement, or friendly feeling; made with the lips alone and without sound, and milder than a laugh
definitions.sun: the star at the centre of the solar system, about which the earth and the other planets revolve, and from which they receive their light and heat; the nearest of the stars to the earth, and by far the brightest body in the sky
definitions.tree: a tall woody plant that lives for many years, having a single main stem or trunk from which branches grow at some height above the ground; distinguished from a shrub by its greater size and its single trunk, and from the soft-stemmed herbs by the wood of which it is formed
definitions.walk: to travel on foot at an ordinary pace, one foot being set down before the other is lifted, so that the body is never wholly unsupported; to go on foot for exercise or pleasure, as distinct from riding or driving
definitions.water: the clear liquid, without color, taste, or smell, that falls as rain, fills rivers, lakes, and seas, and forms the chief constituent of living bodies; also, the compound of hydrogen and oxygen of which this liquid consists
definitions.woman: an adult female human being; a female person who has passed from girlhood to full age; also, the female part of the human race considered collectively
